pages.raw_text:
That American farmers must con-
serve the fertility Of the soil or the
land will pass into the hands Of
large owners, who will see that
more scientific farming is done, was
an assertion Of Prof. w. II. Jardine
soil expert, from the Kansas Agri
cultural college, made at the Dry
Farming congress this morning, and
applauded by that body.


Professor Jardine recited the eX
perience of Europe, where, in most
countries, the bulk of the land has
passed into the hands of large own
ers, who have since forced the ten
ants to preserve and increase the
fertility of the soil until the average
production has increased so per
cent in the last half century. The
American farmer is trying to gel
over too many acres instead of look
ing after each individual acre more
closely.


KEYNOTE OF SESSION.


The conservation of the fertility
of the soil was the keynote of the
session of the Dry Farming con
gress this morning. The speeches
were mostly of an extremely prac
tical nature and received close at
tention. The addresses were by
